title: Dryopteris microlepis

In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Dryopteris microlepis is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which consists of approximately 250 species of ferns. The species is native to China. Dryopteris microlepis is a type of terrestrial fern that grows in a variety of habitats, including forests and grasslands. The classification of Dryopteris microlepis is further categorized under the division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales.
